sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a spear, 2Sa 21:16.

(2) [Cain], pr.n.-(a) of the fratricide son of Adam. Allusion is so made to the etymology in Gen 4:1, that קִין would seem to be the same as קָנָה, “she bare Cain (a creature [rather a possession, see קָנָה ]), and said, I have created [rather possessed or acquired] a man by the help of Jehovah” [of course this is the true derivation].-(b) of the tribe of the Kenites, Numb. 24:22 Jud. 4:11 see קֵינִי .-(c) of a town of the tribe of Judah, with the art. Jos 15:57.
